label: Iran Killed
source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera / Iran Red Crescent
contestNote: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed including 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA: 1,407+ civilian deaths including 214 children. Iran Red Crescent (Mar 29): 1,900–1,937+ killed, 24,800+ injured. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed, ~18,551+ injured. Iran's official floor: ~1,937 killed. WHO: 18 hospitals struck. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 330+ hrs) severely limits verification. Mar 29: IDF struck Tehran neighborhoods (Shaft City, Saadat Abad) — 2 killed, 14 wounded; Tabriz Petrochemical Company hit; University of Science and Technology in Tehran struck. Day 30: strikes on Bushehr nuclear power plant and Haftgel water reservoir.

label: US Killed
deltaNote: 13 KIA confirmed. Total wounded now 300+. Mar 28: Iran struck Prince Sultan AB (Saudi Arabia) — 15 US wounded (5 seriously), refueling aircraft damaged. Mar 29: USS Tripoli arrived with 3,500 additional Marines. 82nd Airborne (~1,000+) deployed to CENTCOM AOR. CENTCOM: Iran's missile launch rate down 90% from Day 1; total 393 attack waves recorded. Apr 6 deadline firm.

label: Israel Killed
source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 19+ killed in Israel total. 4,800+ wounded. Mar 28 (Day 29): Iranian missile struck Eshtaol (Jerusalem area) — IDF interception failure; several lightly injured. Houthi missile toward Beersheba triggered sirens. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rockets; IDF Chief Zamir warns of 15,000-troop shortfall. Iran's pre-stockpiled missiles still penetrating Israeli defenses despite production destroyed.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: 22+ total across Gulf states. Mar 24: Iran struck fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking fire; Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly 'losing patience' and may join strikes on Iran (Bloomberg). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (suspended operations). Qatar LNG remains halted Day 27. Saudi Arabia intercepted 37+ drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
deltaNote: 1,142+ killed including 130+ children; 1M+ displaced. Mar 28: IDF struck Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) and Deir al-Zahrani; Al Manar/Al Mayadeen journalists killed; Lebanese President Aoun condemned strikes. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rocket fire. Mar 25: Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets. IDF has declared 30km security zone to Litani River.

label: Brent Crude
source: Reuters / Al Jazeera / OilPrice.com / CNBC / Goldman Sachs
deltaNote: Brent ~$115/bbl Day 31; spiked on Tabriz Petrochemical strike (Mar 29). Analysts warn of 'April oil cliff' — global SPR buffer expiring mid-April (Rystad Energy, Goldman Sachs, BCA Research). IEA: 'biggest oil shock in history.' ~500M bbl total lost since Feb 28. Apr 6 deadline: Trump will resume strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure if Hormuz not fully reopened. Pakistan secured 20-ship Hormuz deal (2/day).

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Qatar LNG offline (Day 2) — global LNG and fertilizer supply chains disrupted. Hormuz selective passage: Iran allowing China/India/Pakistan-flagged vessels under coordination. 20M bbl/day backed up; 5M diverted to alternate routes. UN FAO emergency alert on food price spike. European natural gas TTF +22% cumulative since war onset. Slovenia: first EU fuel rationing. Spain: €5B emergency energy aid.

label: Countries Affected
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / France 24 / CNN / NewsNation
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia (Prince Sultan AB struck), Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Yemen (Houthis launched), Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France, Switzerland, Pakistan, Slovenia, Thailand, Malaysia (Hormuz passage deals). Day 30-31: Iran struck ALBA Bahrain and EGA Abu Dhabi (aluminium plants); Iranian missile hit Ne'ot Hovav chemical plant in Israel; IRGC threatened university campuses in Gulf; 4-nation Islamabad summit; USS Tripoli (3,500 Marines) arrived. 'April oil cliff' warning issued.
